item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You know, I thought I had given some sort of description of Sector H in this article, but upon re-reading it I realized I hadn't. There's really nothing particularly special about it. It's mainly supposed to be a storage area for safe SCPs and anomalous items that have been deemed unimportant or unable to be researched further. I'll have to add that info somewhere. I'll consider adding more info on Brandt as well, even if it's just a footnote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm not opposed to the idea of a story being carried across multiple skips, but I really wish there was more information in this article about who Brandt is, and what Sector H is for, seeing as they are both necessary components to the event instigated here. From a tonal standpoint, I'm bothered by a Foundation agent acting so blasé about what sounds like a potential breach. It's hard to imagine any workplace where a naked man in an owl mask would be brushed off as drunken hijinks; at a Foundation site, it seems more likely to result in a task force being deployed, than a "probably nothing" type of comment. What is, or was, Sector H, that this kind of activity doesn't result in a higher level of alarm? Never-the-less, there's definitely something or interest to me going on here, and I hope reading the linked articles gives me more than what this little glimpse offered.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I loved 3094 and I especially love this and how you expanded on the story.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It's tricky because a lot of them share similar traits.

+ show spoilers
- hide spoilers

#2 is Aim, and #3 is Shax.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: -1 and -4 outright say they're Andras and Beleth, but what are -2 and -3? I'm not completely sure but I think 2 is Dantalion and 3 is either Shax or Halphas.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I enjoyed the narrative present here, it's a nice story that gives the opportunity to branch of and explore a wider world. The general framework at play here of a compromised Foundation facility has certainly been done before but it's executed here with expert polish.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The image of Stolas is what originally inspired 3094. I wanted to answer the question, "How can an owl with long legs and a crown be intimidating?" A lot of the images for goetic demons are fascinating to me like that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Ars Goetia is great fuel for pretty much any kind of fiction. I approve!

I like Stolas because he is pretty much a Furby on stilts and Paimon because he rides a camel.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This one took some time. It's the first big connection in a story I want to tell, which started with 3094, and has bled into 3344 a bit now. As with 3094, this is based on demons from the Ars Goetia.

Thank you to LadyKatie, CaptainKirby, AnnRKiss, Dysadron, and daveyoufool for their help in critiquing this. Major, major thanks to thegreathippo for helping me with the clinical tone, which I often struggle with. There are a few others I'm certain I'm forgetting, and I apologize for that. If you let me know in the comments, I'll be happy to credit you.
